hotel_name: St. Louis Union Station Hotel, Curio Collection by Hilton

reviews.title: Old hotel that needs updates.
reviews.text:
Check in was slow with only3 people working the desk. The room was dirty we had to call the busy front desk for towels , paper products, and coffee   The valet parking ( the only type of parking available) was slow and unorganized.  It took 1 hour and 40 minutes to get our vehicle to leave 
Overall I would visit the aquarium and ride the gondola wheel but don’t stay at the hotel.

reviews.title: Over priced & don’t book with booking.com you won’t get the room you paid for
reviews.negatives: Hair stuck in our linens that wasn’t ours, room smelled. We paid almost $500 for a room with a garden view which was extra than a regular room and we got a room looking at a roof with ac units.

reviews.title: Hotel forgets everything
reviews.text: Hotel put us on a very long walk to the furthest room very very very far away. If you ask front desk for anything like towels or an extra bed expect to ask 3 or 4 times and they t not show up. Terrible follow through, I get tired of asking for things they said they would bring to me right away. Would not stay here again.

reviews.title: Short visit, run down property with no comforts of a Hitlon Curio
reviews.positives: Lobby and getting into lobby was odd....The lobby to the rooms had a 5min walk...
reviews.negatives: The overall experience wasn't that of a Hilton Curio.  This hotel is dated, the rooms need a refresh and the lobby/conference space was leaking with rain

reviews.title: Worst hotel experience I’ve had in a while
reviews.text: We found articles of clothing in our bed. Our bathroom Floors were dirty. Customer service was impossible. Valet was incredibly unprofessional. Trashcans were overflowing throughout the hotel. Told them about shattered glass in the floor of one of the main areas but it was still there the next day. Was really so excited to stay here and it was just overall an unpleasant experience.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: there was no parking because the parking lot for the hotel was rented out for an event that evening. had to go way down to the union station parking lot to find a spot. front desk wasn’t very helpful and when i called no one answered.

reviews.title: Hotel is costly due to available attractions within.
reviews.text: However, none of the attractions were open during my stay due to city wide curfew and peaceful protests during the day.

reviews.title: I’ll never stay here again.
reviews.positives: Parts of the facility were pretty.
reviews.negatives: Booking.com didn’t mention anything about a $50 “convenience fee” the hotel charges. Its mentioned that parking is available but it wasn’t until I actually hit the link and read the policies that the parking fee of $50, not $45, is charged. I got an email saying our room for one night with 2 queen beds was going to be $296 but at the end of it all, I got charged $396 thanks to these fees. If I had known that upfront, I definitely wouldn’t have stayed there. The room definitely was not worth almost $400. We could hear our neighbors over our tv when they were having casual conversation. There was such a lack of direction to the room that we had no idea where to go until we took a few wrong turns and figured it out. Between the low ceilings and the lack of windows on our way to the room, I thought we were in the basement, but it turned out that we were on the ground floor.

reviews.title: Nice hotel. Inconvenient accommodation.
reviews.text: The hotel was very nice. However during our stay the main lobby area was closed. We wanted to see the light show, but was not able to.  Valet parking lost my keys. The lady at the Valet stand, I believe her name was Jasmine or something like that was rude, condescending. Basically hateful.  The Valet Chris was amazing. He was an older gentleman who went above and beyond. Great person. Back to the hotel.  While nice, with all of the areas shut off from guest for special events it made getting my girlfriends elderly parents to dinner with us very difficult and inconvenient.  I doubt that I will stay here again. Unfortunately

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Room thermostat didn’t work, too far of a walk from parking lot to the hotel. No indoor pool

reviews.title: Accessibility issues galore!
reviews.text: If you are wheelchair or stroller bound, this hotel does not for you. There are very few rams and if you need one you have to go all the way around out of the way. If you are a hotel guests, they make it a burden for you if you don’t do valet. You have to park in a side or back parking lot and walk a quarter of a mile to get to some sort of entrance. It is really hard to find the hotel entrance from the aquarium side, due to a lack of signs. Make sure to lift up on the handles for key card entry doors, they really need to be replaced. When parking on the weekend, a security like to block off parking lot exits. And there are barricades everywhere making you walk even further when the entrance is just right there. This location is not suitable for the handicapped or disabled. Ramps in elevators are a few Can be prepared for a lot of walking to inconvenient parts of the hotel that are not close to your room or any main lobby area. Also, Station Grill online says it’s open for dinner but it’s only open for breakfast and lunch.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives:
You have to pay $40 a day for parking on top of price of room.
Rooms are  mile away. 
Called housekeeping 3 times and they never came. Would not stay here again.
$53 if you want a hamburger at the hotel restaurant

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel lobby
reviews.text: The hotel lobby was fabulous but the rest of the hotel was disappointing. Check in and out was slow. They gave no information nor ask how our stay was - which are just the basic comments you expect. Let alone any additional comments. The tv remote didn’t work but they didn’t fix it (although they said they would). The valet man on arrival was really lovely and helpful although due to poor signage we had to use valet parking because it’s all we could find! I would have liked some information on the history of the train station - especially because this is the selling point. I’m glad I saw the lobby which I’d visit again, but I wouldn’t rush to stay in the hotel.

reviews.title: Don’t stay here
reviews.negatives:
I called the night before requesting housekeeping for the following day. We were gone the following day until 3:00 and housekeeping never came. I called 3 times to request housekeeping, checking is horrible. 
I was not told when you check in that if you park in the parking lot across street that $40 per day gets charged to your room.
I paid for parking with the parking attendant each day we left the parking lot . So I was charged $80 a day for parking😡 rooms smell like chemicals and long walk to your room. I would never stay here again. Oh I forgot if you want a hamburger in the hotel cost is $53

reviews.title: Not worth the price
reviews.text: For the price of this hotel, it wasn’t worth it. This hotel is in a great location- there’s a lot to do in the area, and the lobby is really cool. It is hotel itself is huge, and parking (which is a separate fee) isn’t close to the entrance, so you have to haul your luggage far. There is one main set of elevators, and it’s a long walk to the rooms. Our key cards didn’t work at first, so we had a long walk back to get new cards. When we finally got into the room, there were Doritos in the middle of the floor and between the beds. There was also coffee residue in the sink, dust covering every surface, and a pencil and pretzel on the floor. We ended up getting a new room, which was somewhat cleaner- this one had a plastic lid on the floor and hair in the shower. I asked for housekeeping to come clean, and I was told they needed 24 hours notice. We got the manager involved at this point, and he attempted to make it right. We ended up in a third room, and this one only had a little hair in the shower. Overall, the cleanliness of the rooms was not great- I’m guessing housekeeping is short-staffed. The location and building itself were awesome, but with parking and breakfast being extra, in addition to the already high price of the room, I would choose to stay somewhere else next time.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Outside activities
reviews.negatives: Poor customer service. Stayed on July 3rd I parked with Valet Parking. Called to get my vehicle no one was answering the number they told me to call for pick up. I CALLED THE FRONT DESK. SHE TOLD ME THAT I CALLED THE WRONG NUMBER WELL THE HIGHLIGHTED NUMBER NO ONE ANSWERED SHE HUFFED AS SHE SAID ILL LET THEM KNOW. COME TO FIND OUT They failed to tell me that Valet on the the 4th was moved AND WAS ON THE SIDE OF THW BUILDING.... and the lady at the front had the nerve to give me lip service. I HAD MY 82 YEAR OLD GRANNY WHOM THEN HAD TO WALK AROUND THE BUILDING BECAUSE OF THE INCOMPETENCEY OF THE STAFF AND HIGHLY UNPROFESSIONAL STAFF I THINK HER NAME WAS PRECIOUS OR SOMETHING SHE NEEDS JOB CUSOMER SERVICE COACHING ASAP! The Valet parking people were also getting mad with her because she was getting ignorant with them over the walky as if customers couldn't hear what she was saying!

reviews.title: Fairly neat but not sure worth the cost
reviews.text: It’s okay. Maybe better to just stop by and look around and not stay there d/t how expensive it is. Even with having a room still $35 for parking. Food sitting outside of rooms our whole stay (staff just would walk past and not pick up)

reviews.title: Terrible Hotel Hospitality
reviews.positives: Location and beautiful architecture
reviews.negatives: Staff was rude. They were understaffed. Took over 2 hours to get the towels they forgot to leave in the room. We had to schedule a clean everyday but front desk never answered. We had to go to the pool just to find towels to take a shower. Way over priced! Will not revisit.

reviews.title: A bit disappointing
reviews.text: While this is a beautiful facility, we experienced a let down for the money that we spent. There were two events being held which really limited our access to the facilities. But the real issue was that our room was not ready at check in, and we waited for two hours to gain access to it. This made getting ready for our event in town difficult. There were other people waiting in a very long line and all throughout the lobbies. This should not happen at a facility of this level of expense.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Our room was Very dirty. The towel was dirty,  and hair was still all over bathroom. Hair was on door, shelve, tub wall and ceiling. The door top latch was broken.

reviews.title: Beautiful but not convenient 50/50
reviews.text:
This place is for people who specifically come to view the beautiful building inside and out with it's history and entertaining places near by. It comes with the basics such as a very clean room, nice decent size towels, bigger than usual TV, cureig with options of tea and coffee (no water), higher quality shapoo & conditioner and super comfortable beds. I say "basic"  because that's what it should come with for a hotel room with regular price of 260 dlls.(I got it in spacial here in agoda for a bit over 100dlls 😊)
  Other than that, this place is not convenient for your tipical traveler who stops for convenience. 
I paid over 100dlls for 1 room with 2 queens. They charge you a ridiculous 35 dlls for parking even if you are staying at the hostel's parking.  You basically have to walk around the building for a bit from the parking lot to the main entrence so if you have luggage get ready....
No convenience stores at walking distance nor in the bulding. Prepare for another maze walk in the hotel until you get to your room....
Rooms are decent looking but not the big wow for the price. 
  So at the end of the day it really depends what you are here for. 
It's best suited for people with a car who is willing to pay for a room near known attractions in a gorgeous hotels with a history.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Location was ideal.
reviews.negatives: Gave away the king suites I had booked 2 months in advance and left us with standard doubles but still charged us the king suite rate. Also, main hall with bar and light show was blocked off for a wedding reception...other than location, this had been the main draw of the hotel for us.

reviews.title: Not Worth the Money
reviews.text:
The room we had was definitely not worth the money. We had a king bed with a pull out sofa for our daughter. 
The light and phone didn’t work, the bathtub was unusable because there was no drain stopper, the bathtub was cracked as well, we had no menu to order room service from (and the phone didn’t work anyway). We had requested extra pillows which we never receive. We let the staff know when we checked out (we were there less than 24 hours) but nothing was done about it.

The food in the grand hall was good. They were short staffed so some of it was cold, but still tasty. The salmon and steak were fantastic.

Overall, I would stay somewhere else and just eat there.

reviews.title: pitiful.

reviews.negatives:
old and tired.  does not represent what I expect from Hilton.  Faucet in room was barely attached.  toilet seat was rigged on. Linens were dingy. called the front desk twice and nobody ever answered.  
the Pet area was a joke.  I repeatedly ask in two separate calls about the convenience. I was assured it was.  it was NOT.  At the back of the property. weeds and dirt is what it was. Had its own pets too,rabbits.  really???

reviews.title: the hotel has many activities and nice antiques
reviews.text: I liked the location, the room and other places were dusty. Made me think they hardly clean.

reviews.title: Parking is not free
reviews.text:
I was not told about any free parking, cost me $30 a day not exactly sure what I'm paying for, since I had issues with a key fob in my battery went dead, causing my alarm to go off four times on my car, and no one ever came to inspect. I guess the only reason they have security is to make sure people pay when they go out.
Other than that the hotel and surrounding areas are pretty, and clean. Everything that we were planning to go to was nearby, or at the hotel itself. Was kind of disappointed that the train that they had on display was not viewable for hotel guest, since there are posters, and pictures of the train all over the inside of the room made me think that they would.
Other than that I enjoyed my stay.

reviews.title: Cool Experience
reviews.text: There are many cool and fun aspects of this hotel that make this a place worth staying at! There is a cool light show in the lobby and entertainment in the area for kids and adults to enjoy.

reviews.title: Fabulous building and great location but room itself is not as great
reviews.text: The building is fabulous and there are ferris wheel, mini golf and aquarium just behind it. Also there are fire show and light show every evening which is great! The problem is the cleaness of the room. We found big stain in the sheet and bed cover is dirty. Hope it can improve its cleaness to match this great building.

reviews.title: Nice, but expensive
reviews.text: Nice hotel, staff and stay, but pretty expensive for what you get. Well over $200/night by the time you add $35/day parking. Unless you’re set on staying here, I would recommend staying a cheaper hotel nearby and stopping in for a drink and the light show in the lobby at night, or breakfast in the AM, which would give you the same satisfaction of checking the place out.

reviews.title: OMG!! Amazing!
reviews.text:
Breathing. 
Such history, and beauty.

reviews.title: Great Location
reviews.text: The staff was great! We did however have a few issues. First we chose to do our own parking and the lot was full. We had to go to a back lot. Our room was nice and big but not cleaned well. There was still a party  favor behind the entertainment center. The mini fridge didn't work, and there was some sort of urine, vomit stain around the toilet. It was very convenient staying at the Hotel because the next morning we went to the Aquarium. Even though our room was at the end the music and light show didn't bother us.

reviews.title: Great concert local - some wear and tear
reviews.text: Great Concert Location a couple blocks to Enterprise Center - Some wear and tear (missing closet handle and loose door security chain/bar lock). One of two key cards didn't work and had to walk back to the desk. Historic parts of the hotel are awesome.  The modern add-on part of the hotel under the Union Station awning has shorter ceiling, long maze with only one set of elevators instead of two for each wing.  Was quiet for having a bazillion female pop concert goers.  Pool and landscaping are great except is a bit small for size hotel.  Do note the STL Aquarium, soda pop store, and giant ferris wheel are all under construction till 2019 winter maybe fall so request to be on the side away from the construction it's a way longer walk from the one set of elevators but I think it is worth it.  Station Grill restaurant food and staff were awesome and highly professional.  Beds are super soft.  Bathroom was large.  The bedroom was quite small and cozi.  HVAC worked well in the heat. Hotel has room service.  There is an Irish pub and restaurant next door if you want cheaper food.  Hotel staff seems to be able to handle large numbers of people checking in and out all 10 stations were staffed.

reviews.title: Friendly staff
reviews.text: The staff was friendly. They let me know of all the attractions that my kids might enjoy. The should probably update the elevators soon but other than that we enjoyed our stay.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: Room was very spacious and clean. We were supposed to be in one of the courtyard rooms but still not available at the time, but still given a nice room with a good view. Breakfast was confusing because when I called front desk, they told me go to the restaurant breakfast and I did not know you had to pay there. We were supposed to just take the regular breakfast which is on the other side of the restaurant. Had to approach front desk, I was told to go to the other breakfast location that’s included in our booking. Went there, tapped my card and was not working. Had to go back to front desk and the other lady told me I don’t have breakfast privileges and then she later discovered we did have breakfast.  3 different people telling me 3 different things. That’s the only downside that I did not like. And maybe, if it’s a 4-star hotel, they should have upgraded the breakfast options that they have, there’s no difference than other hotels. Overall stay was okay. And lastly, no microwaves in the rooms.

reviews.title: Staying at Union Station!
reviews.text: We had an amazing weekend!! The location was great, there is plants to do at Union Station itself and it pretty closed to much more in downtown St Louis. Our room was fine but they could be better cleaning, there were left behind prescription pills near the night stand, we had to move the bed a bit to get something that fell back and it was pretty gross back there. On surface level it was clean, the bathroom was clean and the sheets looked good.

reviews.title: Super excited to stay. Disappointed while we were there. Super excited to leave.
reviews.positives: Location was good. Exterior was beautiful
reviews.negatives: Got double charged for parking. Did not recieve the room I booked. The shower head was about to fall off, mini fridge had food from the previous guests in it. Coffee maker did not have cups for us to use.

reviews.title: Way over priced and quality going way downhill, so sad.
reviews.positives: Beautiful historic building, light show in bar area.
reviews.negatives: Our room didn't look like it was ever vacuumed, beds were very uncomfortable, thermostat was on 76 when we arrived and it stayed incredibly warm all night despite changing it, woke up to a barking dog at 6am, and there was a sewer smell in many common areas and the restaurants. We had bad experiences at both restaurants on property which were dirty with long waits and poor service. Our experience last year was much better which is why we returned this year. Overall I just feel we paid a lot of money for a bad experience. Staff reached out and I told them ALL of this - no response. Very disappointing. AVOID, this is becoming a Christmas tourist trap.

reviews.title: Stay at Drury Inn across the street!
reviews.positives: Location. But you are better off staying at the Drury Inn across the street(you get breakfast and two drink tickets per adult per day during your stay at Drury Inn.
reviews.negatives: We were treated like criminals. We had come back to our room from swimming and two security guards were standing outside our room like the fbi all because we were from out of state and out payment did not go thru. Even though when we checked in they said everything was fine. They followed one security guard in front and one behind my fiancé. All for a mistake they wouldn't have caught had I not gone to the front desk for them charging two of our cards for incidentals. Our keycards didn't work half the time. And this happened with a lot of guests. The bar said they served food from the kitchen until 11 and at 1050 we tried to order and they said the kitchen was closed. This wouldn't had mattered if our DoorDash order was not messed up right before this. Trying to feed 3 adults and kids. No snack vending machines. Leaks everywhere. You have to pay $35/night for parking! Again location was cool-everything else-not so much

reviews.title: Dirty rooms and wasted my money
reviews.positives: The lady at the counter was helpful and nice.
reviews.negatives: The room we originally got was dirty. There was soda or something splattered on the wall and the floor wasn't vacuumed. The counter in the bathroom was covered in hair. I requested a new room and they gave us a room with one bed instead of the 2 I paid for. I didn't have time to move again because our event was about to begin so we stayed in the new room. When returning that evening we noticed the new room was even more dirty. The floors weren't vacuumed and had toe nail clippings on it. The bed had yellow stains on the linens and worst of all it looked like someone whipped poop on the curtains! I also noticed the outlets were extremely loose and I had to hold my phone charger in them for it to charge my phone. It was a night I was excited for and it was a pretty big let down.

reviews.title: An extremely fun and entertaining stay for the entire family.
reviews.positives:
The historic building property of St. Louis Union Station Hotel, Curio by Hilton, was an almost perfect choice for my family to stay the night while enjoying the local sights, foods and experiences before we took the short train ride (right outside of Union Station) directly to our Cardinals & Dodgers baseball game, at Busch Stadium.

The majority of Union Station’s architectural exterior and larger interior public rooms and spaces have been restored and preserved to their former glory, making it a unique hotel property within downtown St. Louis.

The back parking area (which used to be the entrance to the shopping area of Union Station between its public transportation life and its now lodging and hospitality life) now welcomes visitors with an ice cream and soda fountain grille (and lots of candy) as you enter the Union Station Hotel property.

As you walk passed the grille, you are greeted by a massive entertainment, shopping and restaurant village-like area, the entirety of which has a semi open-air (partially-covered) roofing structure that allows just enough sun and weather through to allow for that authentic outside feeling while still protecting you from the hot Summer heat.

Within this complex, there is a large Koi pond, stocked with different fish, some up to 200 years old. Along the pond, on either side, are restaurants and plenty of outdoor seating and tables to enjoy the weather, watching your children play or just to relax.

Walk up a few short steps passed the grille and you will enter into an East Coast-like boardwalk atmosphere, complete with upscale seafood restaurant, Landry’s, and all kinds of activities and entertainment for your children or grandchildren: tossing balls into hoops, darts and balloons, football, popcorn, candy, and anything else your little ones could want.

To one side of the games (although seasonal) are real Pullman train cars - one of which is designated The Polar Express (we will be going back during Christmas).
reviews.negatives:
The weekend we stayed, there happened to be a large wedding and wedding party there, in one of the massive public rooms, on-site. I get it - we’ve all been to weddings and understand the many variables that seem to pop-up and veer intended plans off-course. It just happened to overwhelm the staff this particular weekend. By no means would I call it bad service, but they were definitely spread thin: 300+ wedding guests and a wedding party, switching rooms, cleaning and turning-over rooms to new guests is a lot of work. 

Again, it wasn’t bad service…maybe just service delayed due to an inordinately large wedding party. 
The small things that always make the difference in hotel service seemed to suffer from the overbooking (or possible understaffing) of the wedding party: multiple luggage mistakes (first, not arriving to the room at all, then arriving to the wrong room, no early check-in and early kitchen closing (for a St. Louis hotel) were our only issues. And 2 of those 3 were understandable due to the wedding. 

We had a great time at a very fine property.
